items.headline: ADO work items are a prompt injection surface
items.body: Microsoft's Copilot Cowork was sending emails without user approval; prompt injection via external content caused messages to include attacker-controlled images, leaking pre-authenticated OneDrive download links. The attack surface is identical to what ADO MCP Server faces — work item descriptions and PR comments are user-controlled content that flows directly into Claude's context. Worth auditing whether any ADO MCP tool calls make external-facing changes without an explicit confirmation step, and whether any tool responses include resource URLs that shouldn't leave the session.
